items.title: Ayushman Bharat (PM-JAY)
items.description: Provides free health coverage up to 5 lakhs per family for secondary and tertiary care hospitalization.
steps: Check name in the SECC-2011 database or PM-JAY list.
Visit a nearby empanelled hospital or CSC center.
Verify identity using Aadhaar or Ration Card.
Collect your Ayushman Golden Card for cashless treatment.
items.eligibility: Low-income families listed in SECC-2011 or holding specific Ration Cards.
items.where_to_apply: Common Service Center (CSC) or Empanelled Hospital
items.notes: Always carry the Golden Card during admission for cashless benefits.

items.title: Maternal Healthcare (J SY)
items.description: Financial assistance for pregnant women to promote institutional delivery in government hospitals.
steps: Register pregnancy at the nearest health center within 12 weeks.
Obtain an MCP (Mother and Child Protection) card.
Complete at least 4 antenatal check-ups.
Deliver baby in a government facility to receive cash incentive.
items.eligibility: All pregnant women over 19 years for first two live births.
items.notes: Ensure bank account is linked to Aadhaar for direct benefit transfer.

items.title: Child Vaccination (Indradhanush)
items.description: Free vaccination for children and pregnant women against life-threatening diseases.
steps: Visit nearest health center on designated 'Village Health Days'.
Present the child's birth certificate or MCP card.
Receive the vaccine according to the age schedule.
Update the vaccination card for future visits.
items.eligibility: Infants and children up to 12 years; pregnant women.
items.notes: Keep the vaccination card safe as it is required for school admissions.

items.title: Free Diagnostic Services
items.description: Free essential diagnostic tests available at government healthcare facilities.
steps: Consult a doctor at a government hospital.
Obtain a prescription for the required tests.
Visit the hospital lab or radiology department.
Collect reports within the specified timeframe.
items.eligibility: All citizens visiting government health facilities.
items.notes: Some high-end tests like MRI may require a prior appointment.

items.title: Mental Health Support (Tele-MANAS)
items.description: 24/7 free tele-mental health services and professional counselling.
steps: Dial the toll-free number 14416.
Select your preferred language.
Speak with a trained counselor regarding your concerns.
Receive a referral to a specialist if necessary.
items.eligibility: Open to all citizens.
items.notes: The service is completely confidential and free of cost.

items.title: Disability Certificate (UDID)
items.description: A single document that provides identity and disability verification for government benefits.
steps: Register online on the Swavlamban Card portal.
Upload photo and disability documents.
Choose the nearest government hospital for assessment.
Visit the hospital on the scheduled date for medical examination.
items.eligibility: Persons with a disability of 40% or more.
items.notes: The UDID card is valid across all states in India.

items.title: TB Treatment (Nikshay)
items.description: Free diagnosis, medicine, and nutritional support for TB patients.
steps: Visit a government hospital for sputum testing.
Register under the Nikshay portal upon positive diagnosis.
Start the DOTS treatment regimen.
Provide bank details for the Rs. 500 monthly nutrition incentive.
items.eligibility: Any person diagnosed with Tuberculosis.
items.notes: Do not stop the medication midway without consulting a doctor.

items.title: Senior Citizen Health Checkups
items.description: Specialized health screening and OPD services for citizens above 60 years.
steps: Visit the dedicated Senior Citizen counter at the hospital.
Register for a health screening card.
Undergo routine checks for BP, Sugar, and vision.
Consult the geriatric specialist.
items.eligibility: Indian citizens aged 60 years and above.
items.notes: Most hospitals have a separate queue for senior citizens.

items.title: Blood Bank Services
items.description: Access to safe blood and blood components through government-run blood banks.
steps: Check blood availability on the eRaktKosh portal.
Obtain a blood requisition form from the hospital.
Provide a replacement donor if required by hospital policy.
Collect blood units after cross-matching.
items.eligibility: Any patient in need of transfusion.
items.notes: Voluntary donors receive a certificate that can be used for future priority.

items.title: Jan Aushadhi (Generic Medicine)
items.description: Quality generic medicines available at much lower prices than branded ones.
steps: Take your doctor's prescription to a Jan Aushadhi Kendra.
Ask for the generic equivalent of the prescribed medicine.
Verify the price and expiry date.
Purchase the medicine at a subsidized rate.
items.eligibility: Open to all citizens.
items.notes: Medicines are 50% to 90% cheaper than market prices.

items.title: Pre-Matric Scholarship (SC/ST/OBC)
items.description: Financial help for students in classes 1 to 10 to reduce school dropout rates.
steps: Register on the National Scholarship Portal (NSP).
Upload income and caste certificates.
Submit application for school verification.
Track status until the amount is credited to the bank.
items.eligibility: Students from reserved categories with family income below specified limits.
items.notes: Ensure the bank account is in the student's name or a joint account with parents.

items.title: Post-Matric Scholarship
items.description: Assistance for students pursuing Class 11, 12, Degree, or PG courses.
steps: Apply via the National Scholarship Portal (NSP) or State portal.
Upload previous year marksheet and fee receipts.
Get the application verified by the College Head/Principal.
Wait for District Officer approval.
items.eligibility: Students enrolled in recognized colleges with required attendance.
items.notes: Renew the scholarship every year to continue receiving benefits.

items.title: RTE Free School Admission
items.description: 25% reservation in private schools for children from weaker sections.
steps: Check the RTE admission notification for your state.
Register child's details on the state RTE portal.
Select nearby private schools from the list.
Participate in the lottery system and verify documents if selected.
items.eligibility: Children aged 6-14 from economically weaker sections.
items.notes: Applications usually open between February and April.

items.title: PM Shri Schools
items.description: Enrollment in centrally upgraded schools providing high-quality infrastructure and learning.
steps: Identify the nearest PM Shri designated school in your block.
Visit the school office during the admission window.
Submit the standard admission form.
Attend the orientation for parents and students.
items.eligibility: Students eligible for standard school grades (Primary/Secondary).
items.notes: These schools focus on modern technology and skill-based learning.

items.title: National Means-cum-Merit Scholarship (NMMS)
items.description: Scholarship for meritorious students of government schools based on an entrance exam.
steps: Apply for the NMMS exam through the school in Class 8.
Prepare for the Mental Ability Test (MAT) and Scholastic Aptitude Test (SAT).
Appear for the state-level examination.
If selected, open a bank account for scholarship transfers.
items.eligibility: Class 8 students in govt/aided schools with income < 3.5 Lakhs.
items.notes: Students must maintain at least 55% marks in subsequent classes to continue.

items.title: Central Sector Scheme for University Students
items.description: Scholarship for students who are in the top 20th percentile of their 12th board results.
steps: Apply on the National Scholarship Portal after 12th results.
Provide the board roll number for verification.
Submit college admission proof.
Complete the Aadhaar-based biometric authentication.
items.eligibility: Above 80th percentile in Class 12 and family income < 4.5 Lakhs.
items.notes: Professional course students (Medical/Engineering) are also eligible.

items.title: SWAYAM Free Online Courses
items.description: Free online courses and certifications from top professors at IITs and IIMs.
steps: Visit the SWAYAM portal or download the app.
Register using an email ID.
Join a course and watch the weekly video lectures.
Register for the optional exam to get a verified certificate.
items.eligibility: Anyone (students, teachers, or professionals).
items.notes: The learning is free; only a small fee is charged for the final certificate exam.

items.title: Vidyalakshmi Education Loan
items.description: A single portal for students to apply for education loans to multiple banks.
steps: Register on the Vidyalakshmi portal.
Fill out the Common Education Loan Application Form (CELAF).
Search for loan schemes provided by various banks.
Upload required academic and income documents.
items.eligibility: Students who have secured admission to professional/technical courses.
items.notes: Interest subsidy is available for students from economically weaker sections.

items.title: Kasturba Gandhi Balika Vidyalaya (KGBV)
items.description: Residential schools for girls from disadvantaged groups in backward blocks.
steps: Visit the District Education Office or nearest KGBV school.
Fill out the admission form for residential schooling.
Provide category and age proof.
Wait for the admission list based on vacancy.
items.eligibility: Girls (Classes 6-12) from SC/ST/OBC/Minority or BPL families.
items.notes: Education, lodging, and food are provided completely free of charge.

items.title: National Apprenticeship Training (NATS)
items.description: One-year program providing practical training to fresh graduates and diploma holders.
steps: Register on the NATS portal.
Enter educational details and upload a resume.
Apply for apprenticeship vacancies in public or private sectors.
Attend interviews and join to receive a monthly stipend.
items.eligibility: Graduates or Diploma holders in technical or non-technical fields.
items.notes: This program helps in gaining industry experience and improves job prospects.

items.title: MGNREGA Job Card
items.description: Guaranteed 100 days of wage employment in a financial year to a rural household.
steps: Visit the local Gram Panchayat office.
Submit an application for registration (oral or written).
Gram Panchayat will verify details and issue the Job Card.
Submit a work demand request to get employment within 15 days.
items.eligibility: Adult members of rural households willing to do unskilled manual work.
items.notes: Wages are directly credited to the linked bank account.

items.title: PM Kaushal Vikas Yojana (PMKVY)
items.description: Free skill training and certification to make youth employable.
steps: Find a PMKVY training center near you.
Choose a job role/course (e.g., Electronics, Healthcare).
Complete the training and pass the assessment.
Receive a certificate and monetary reward/placement assistance.
items.eligibility: Unemployed youth or school/college dropouts.
items.notes: The training is provided by government-affiliated centers only.

items.title: Employment Exchange Registration
items.description: Registration with the state employment exchange to receive job notifications and priority.
steps: Visit the state employment exchange portal or office.
Enter educational qualifications and experience.
Upload certificates for verification.
Download the Employment Card with your unique registration number.
items.eligibility: Any citizen seeking employment, aged 18 and above.
items.notes: Renew your registration every 3 years to keep it active.

items.title: PMEGP Loan for Business
items.description: Credit-linked subsidy scheme for setting up new micro-enterprises.
steps: Apply on the PMEGP e-portal.
Submit a project report for your proposed business.
Selected applications are sent to banks for loan approval.
Attend mandatory EDP (Entrepreneurship Development) training.
items.eligibility: Individuals above 18 years; at least 8th pass for large projects.
items.notes: Subsidies range from 15% to 35% of the total project cost.

items.title: PM-SVANidhi (Street Vendor Loan)
items.description: Collateral-free working capital loan of up to ₹10,000 for street vendors.
steps: Apply through the SVANidhi portal or a CSC.
Provide the Letter of Recommendation (LoR) from the municipality.
Bank processes the application and disburses the amount.
Repay on time to become eligible for a higher loan amount.
items.eligibility: Street vendors vending in urban areas on or before March 24, 2020.
items.notes: Interest subsidy is available for timely repayment.

items.title: NCS Job Portal
items.description: A digital platform connecting job seekers with employers across various sectors.
steps: Register as a 'Job Seeker' on the NCS.gov.in portal.
Complete your profile with skills and location preferences.
Search for jobs and apply directly to employers.
Participate in job fairs organized through the portal.
items.eligibility: Anyone looking for a job or career counselling.
items.notes: NCS also offers free career assessment tests and video profiles.

items.title: Deen Dayal Upadhyaya Grameen Kaushalya Yojana (DDU-GKY)
items.description: Placement-linked skill training program specifically for rural poor youth.
steps: Register at a DDU-GKY training center or through the Gram Panchayat.
Undergo a screening process for the chosen trade.
Complete the residential/non-residential training program.
Get placed in a company and receive a completion certificate.
items.eligibility: Rural youth aged 15-35 years from poor families.
items.notes: This scheme focuses on 100% placement for successful candidates.

items.title: National Apprenticeship Promotion Scheme (NAPS)
items.description: Earn while you learn through apprenticeship in various industries with government stipends.
steps: Register on the Apprenticeship India portal.
Search for apprenticeship opportunities in your desired industry.
Apply online and attend the interview.
Sign an apprenticeship contract and start training.
items.eligibility: Anyone aged 14 and above with minimum 5th class education.
items.notes: Government pays a part of the stipend directly to the apprentice.

items.title: Stand-Up India (SC/ST/Women)
items.description: Bank loans between 10 lakhs and 1 crore for setting up a greenfield enterprise.
steps: Visit the Stand-Up India portal or a bank branch.
Select 'Trainee Borrower' or 'Ready Borrower' status.
Prepare a detailed business plan.
Submit the loan application with required collateral/guarantees.
items.eligibility: SC/ST and/or Women entrepreneurs above 18 years.
items.notes: At least 51% shareholding should be with an SC/ST or Woman entrepreneur.

items.title: Mudra Loan (PMMY)
items.description: Loans for non-corporate, non-farm small/micro enterprises.
steps: Identify the loan category: Shishu (up to 50k), Kishore (up to 5L), Tarun (up to 10L).
Fill out the Mudra application form available at banks.
Provide business registration or proof of setup.
Bank evaluates the proposal and sanctions the loan.
items.eligibility: Any Indian citizen with a business plan for non-farm income activities.
items.notes: No collateral or processing fees for Shishu category loans.

items.title: Free Legal Aid (NALSA)
items.description: Legal assistance provided free of cost to the weaker sections of society.
steps: Visit the District Legal Services Authority (DLSA) office.
Fill out a simple application form explaining your case.
Submit proof of eligibility (Income/Caste certificate).
An empanelled lawyer will be assigned to your case.
items.eligibility: Women, children, SC/ST, and persons with income below specified limits.
items.notes: Available for both civil and criminal matters.

items.title: Consumer Complaint Filing
items.description: Seek redressal for defective goods or deficient services through consumer commissions.
steps: Send a formal notice to the seller giving them 15 days to respond.
If unresolved, file a complaint on the 'e-Daakhil' portal.
Pay the nominal court fee (if applicable).
Attend the hearing on the scheduled date.
items.eligibility: Any consumer who has purchased goods or services.
items.notes: You do not necessarily need a lawyer to file a consumer complaint.

items.title: Women's Helpline (181)
items.description: Emergency response and legal support for women facing violence or distress.
steps: Call 181 from any mobile or landline.
Explain the situation to the operator.
The team will coordinate with police, medical, or legal experts.
Visit a One Stop Centre (OSC) for physical support if needed.
items.eligibility: All women and girls in distress.
items.notes: Services are available 24/7 across most states.

items.title: Right to Information (RTI)
items.description: Empowers citizens to request information from public authorities.
steps: Identify the public authority (Department) holding the information.
Write a clear application with specific questions.
Pay the ₹10 fee (postal order or online).
Information must be provided within 30 days.
items.eligibility: Any Indian citizen.
items.notes: BPL applicants are exempt from the RTI fee.

items.title: Senior Citizen Protection (Maintenance Act)
items.description: Legal right for parents and senior citizens to claim maintenance from their children.
steps: Apply at the Maintenance Tribunal (Sub-Divisional Magistrate office).
Explain the lack of support or maintenance.
Tribunal will issue notice to children/relatives.
Tribunal may order a monthly allowance up to ₹10,000 or more.
items.eligibility: Parents or senior citizens unable to maintain themselves.
items.notes: Lawyers are not allowed to represent parties in the Maintenance Tribunal.

items.title: Child Protection (Childline 1098)
items.description: Free phone service for children in need of aid and assistance.
steps: Dial 1098 from any phone.
Provide the location and nature of the concern (e.g., child labor).
Childline team will reach the spot for rescue.
The child is produced before the Child Welfare Committee (CWC).
items.eligibility: Any child below 18 years or a citizen reporting a child's plight.
items.notes: 1098 is a 24-hour national helpline.

items.title: Lok Adalat
items.description: An alternative forum where cases pending in courts are settled amicably.
steps: Submit an application to the court where the case is pending.
Alternatively, apply to the Legal Services Authority for pre-litigation cases.
Attend the Lok Adalat session.
Sign the settlement if both parties agree; the decree is final.
items.eligibility: Parties willing to settle disputes (Civil, Matrimonial, Land, etc.).
items.notes: There is no court fee in Lok Adalat; if a pending case is settled, the original court fee is refunded.

items.title: One Stop Centre (Sakhi)
items.description: Integrated support for women affected by violence, including medical, legal, and shelter.
steps: Visit the Sakhi centre located in the district.
Speak with the administrator or counselor.
Avail medical aid or temporary shelter (up to 5 days).
Receive legal guidance or police assistance on-site.
items.eligibility: Any woman facing any form of violence.
items.notes: The center provides a safe space for women to recover and seek justice.

items.title: Cyber Crime Reporting
items.description: Report financial frauds, social media crimes, and cyber bullying.
steps: Visit cybercrime.gov.in or call 1930.
Register as a user and file a complaint.
Upload screenshots of the fraud and bank transaction details.
Submit the complaint to the nearest cyber police station.
items.eligibility: Any victim of online crime.
items.notes: Reporting within the 'Golden Hour' (first 2 hours) increases the chance of recovering lost money.

items.title: Victim Compensation Scheme
items.description: Financial assistance to victims or their dependents who have suffered loss or injury due to crime.
steps: File an FIR at the police station.
Apply for compensation at the District Legal Services Authority (DLSA).
Submit the medical reports and FIR copy.
DLSA will conduct an enquiry and award compensation.
items.eligibility: Victims of specified crimes where the offender is not identified or for rehabilitation.
items.notes: Compensation is provided for medical expenses and loss of livelihood.

items.title: Ration Card Application
items.description: Get a Ration Card to purchase subsidized food grains from Fair Price Shops.
steps: Obtain the application form from the Taluk/Food office or Akshaya.
Attach family photos and address proof.
Submit for field verification by the Food Inspector.
Collect the Ration Card once approved.
items.eligibility: Families residing in the state (categories: APL/BPL/Antyodaya).
items.notes: Keep the mobile number updated for OTP-based grain collection.

items.title: Old Age Pension (IGNOAPS)
items.description: Monthly financial assistance for senior citizens from BPL families.
steps: Fill out the pension application form at the Panchayat or Municipality.
Submit age and BPL status proof.
Verification by the Gram Panchayat or Social Welfare Officer.
Amount will be credited monthly to the linked bank account.
items.eligibility: Citizens aged 60 years or above belonging to a BPL household.
items.notes: The amount varies from state to state.

items.title: PM Awas Yojana (Rural/Urban)
items.description: Financial assistance to build a permanent house for homeless or those living in kutcha houses.
steps: Register on the PMAY portal or via the local body.
Upload details of current living condition.
Wait for the field survey and inclusion in the beneficiary list.
Receive funds in installments as construction progresses.
items.eligibility: Families with no permanent house and income within limits.
items.notes: Beneficiary must contribute a small portion of the construction cost.

items.title: PM Kisan Samman Nidhi
items.description: Direct income support of ₹6,000 per year in three installments to farmer families.
steps: Register on the PM Kisan portal or via a CSC.
Upload landholding documents (Patta/Chitta).
Verify the bank account with Aadhaar (e-KYC).
Check status online for installment credits.
items.eligibility: Farmer families with cultivable land in their name.
items.notes: e-KYC is mandatory to receive the installments.

items.title: Ujjwala Yojana (Free Gas)
items.description: Deposit-free LPG connection for women from poor households.
steps: Visit the nearest LPG distributor (Indane, HP, Bharat).
Submit the Ujjwala application form.
Choose between 14.2kg or 5kg cylinder.
Wait for document verification and stove/cylinder delivery.
items.eligibility: Adult women from BPL or specific categories (SC/ST/forest dwellers).
items.notes: Refills are available at subsidized rates.

items.title: Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) Linking
items.description: Enabling your bank account to receive government subsidies directly.
steps: Visit your bank branch with Aadhaar Card.
Fill out the DBT linking/NPCI mapping form.
Submit the form and get an acknowledgement.
Check status on the Aadhaar website.
items.eligibility: Any citizen having a bank account and Aadhaar.
items.notes: Only one bank account can be active for DBT at a time.

items.title: Widow Pension Scheme
items.description: Financial support for women after the death of their husband.
steps: Collect the application from the Social Welfare Department or portal.
Submit the death certificate of the husband.
Verification by the local revenue officer.
Monthly pension credit starts after approval.
items.eligibility: Widows aged 18-79 living below the poverty line.
items.notes: Pension stops if the woman remarries or crosses the income limit.

items.title: LPG Subsidy Status Check
items.description: Check if you are receiving the subsidy amount in your bank account for LPG refills.
steps: Go to mylpg.in portal.
Select your gas provider.
Click on 'Give your feedback online'.
Enter your mobile or LPG ID to view the subsidy history.
documents_required: LPG ID or Registered Mobile Number
items.eligibility: Registered LPG consumers.
items.notes: If not receiving subsidy, check if Aadhaar is linked to the gas ID.

items.title: Birth Registration
items.description: Official recording of a birth to obtain a Birth Certificate.
steps: Ensure the hospital records the birth in their register.
Collect the 'Form 1' or discharge slip from the hospital.
Visit the Municipality/Panchayat office within 21 days.
Collect the certificate after registration.
items.eligibility: Parents of the newborn.
items.notes: Registration is free within the first 21 days of birth.

items.title: Fertilizer Subsidy (DBT in Fertilizer)
items.description: Provides fertilizers to farmers at subsidized rates through POS machines.
steps: Visit a licensed fertilizer retailer.
Provide your Aadhaar number or Kisan Credit Card.
Perform biometric authentication on the POS machine.
Purchase the fertilizer at the subsidized price.
items.eligibility: All farmers.
items.notes: The subsidy is paid by the government directly to the manufacturer based on your purchase.

items.title: Aadhaar Card Enrollment
items.description: Unique 12-digit identity number for all Indian residents.
steps: Visit an authorized Aadhaar Enrollment Center.
Fill out the enrollment form.
Provide biometric data (fingerprints, iris scan) and photo.
Collect the acknowledgment slip and wait for the card by post.
items.eligibility: All residents of India (including infants).
items.notes: Keep your mobile number linked to Aadhaar for online services.

items.title: PAN Card (Permanent Account Number)
items.description: Required for financial transactions and filing income tax returns.
steps: Apply online via NSDL or UTIITSL website.
Select Form 49A (for Indian citizens).
Upload documents or use Aadhaar-based e-KYC.
Pay the processing fee and receive the card by post.
items.eligibility: Any individual, minor (through parents), or company.
items.notes: Instant e-PAN can be generated for free using Aadhaar on the Income Tax portal.

items.title: Voter ID Card (EPIC)
items.description: Enables citizens to vote in elections and serves as identity proof.
steps: Apply on the Voter Portal (NVSP) or use the Voter Helpline App.
Fill out 'Form 6' for new registration.
Upload photo and age proof.
Booth Level Officer (BLO) will visit for physical verification.
items.eligibility: Indian citizens aged 18 years or above.
items.notes: Check your name in the electoral roll before every election.

items.title: Income Certificate
items.description: An official document certifying the annual income of a family.
steps: Apply online through the State's e-District portal or at a CSC.
Provide salary slips or an affidavit regarding income.
Village Officer/Tehsildar will conduct an enquiry.
Download the digitally signed certificate.
items.eligibility: Any resident needing proof of income for schemes.
items.notes: The certificate is usually valid for one year or one financial year.

items.title: Caste Certificate
items.description: Proof of belonging to a particular caste, used for reservation and scholarships.
steps: Apply at the local Tehsildar office or via e-District portal.
Provide father’s/relative’s caste certificate as proof.
Field verification by the Revenue Inspector.
Certificate is issued after approval by the Tehsildar.
items.eligibility: Citizens belonging to SC, ST, or OBC categories.
items.notes: OBC certificates may require a 'Non-Creamy Layer' status for central government jobs.

items.title: Driving Licence (Learner's)
items.description: A temporary license valid for 6 months to learn driving.
steps: Apply online on the Sarathi Parivahan portal.
Book an appointment for the computer-based learner's test.
Pass the test (rules and signs).
Download the Learner's Licence immediately.
items.eligibility: Aged 16 (for gearless) or 18 (for geared/LMV).
items.notes: You can apply for a permanent license 30 days after the LL is issued.

items.title: Passport (Fresh)
items.description: Required for international travel and acts as strong identity proof.
steps: Register on the Passport Seva portal.
Fill the application form and pay the fee online.
Book an appointment at a PSK or POPSK.
Complete physical verification and police verification.
items.eligibility: All Indian citizens.
items.notes: Ensure your address matches your documents for smooth police verification.

items.title: Birth Certificate (Late Registration)
items.description: Registering a birth that was not recorded within the first 21 days.
steps: Obtain a 'Non-Availability Certificate' from the Municipality.
File an affidavit before a Magistrate.
Get an order from the Magistrate for late registration.
Submit the order to the Registrar to get the certificate.
items.eligibility: Any citizen whose birth was not registered.
items.notes: Late registration usually involves a penalty fee.

items.title: Domicile/Residence Certificate
items.description: Proves that a person is a resident of a particular state/UT.
steps: Apply through the e-District portal or CSC.
Submit proof of residing in the state for a specified number of years.
Verification by the local revenue officer.
Download the certificate online.
items.eligibility: Residents living in the state for a long period (usually 10-15 years).
items.notes: Required for state government jobs and local quota in colleges.

items.title: Life Certificate (Jeevan Pramaan)
items.description: A digital document for pensioners to prove they are alive to continue receiving pension.
steps: Visit a CSC or use the Jeevan Pramaan app on a biometric-enabled phone.
Provide Aadhaar and Pension Payment Order (PPO) number.
Authenticate using a fingerprint or iris scan.
The certificate is automatically sent to the Pension Disbursing Agency.
items.eligibility: Retired government employees and other pensioners.
items.notes: This must be submitted every year, usually in the month of November.

items.title: Student Bus Pass
items.description: Discounted or free bus travel for students in government and private schools/colleges.
steps: Obtain a bus pass application form from the school or bus depot.
Get the form attested by the Principal/Headmaster.
Submit the form at the nearest State Transport (RTC) counter.
Pay the nominal fee and collect the pass.
items.eligibility: Full-time students of recognized educational institutions.
items.notes: The pass is usually valid for one academic year or semester.

items.title: Senior Citizen Bus Concession
items.description: Discounted bus fares for elderly citizens in state-run transport buses.
steps: Visit the RTC bus depot with age proof.
Register for a Senior Citizen ID Card.
Show the card to the conductor while purchasing tickets.
Pay the discounted fare (usually 25% to 50% off).
items.eligibility: Citizens aged 60 years or above.
items.notes: In some states, travel is completely free for senior citizens.

items.title: Train Ticket Booking (General)
items.description: Process to book train tickets for travel across India via IRCTC.
steps: Register on the IRCTC website or app.
Search for trains between your source and destination.
Enter passenger details and choose class (Sleeper, 3AC, etc.).
Complete payment and download the M-ticket.
items.eligibility: Any citizen.
items.notes: Always carry a valid original ID proof while travelling.

items.title: Vehicle Registration (RC)
items.description: Official registration of a new motor vehicle with the RTO.
steps: Vehicle dealer usually initiates the process online.
Pay the road tax and registration fee.
Take the vehicle to the RTO for inspection by the MVI.
RC is sent to the owner's address by post.
items.eligibility: Any vehicle owner.
items.notes: Registration is valid for 15 years for private vehicles.

items.title: Metro Rail Smart Card
items.description: A rechargeable card for seamless travel in city metro systems.
steps: Visit the Customer Care counter at any Metro station.
Pay the security deposit and initial top-up amount.
Tap the card at the entry gate to travel.
Recharge at kiosks or online when the balance is low.
items.eligibility: Open to all commuters.
items.notes: Smart card users often get a 10% discount on every trip.

items.title: High Security Registration Plate (HSRP)
items.description: Mandatory tamper-proof number plates for all motor vehicles.
steps: Book HSRP online through the authorized state portal.
Choose your vehicle brand and RTO/Dealer location.
Pay the fee and select an appointment slot.
Visit the dealer to get the plate fixed on the scheduled date.
items.eligibility: All vehicle owners with old plates.
items.notes: Do not buy plates from local shops; only authorized HSRP is legal.

items.title: Railway Concession for PWD
items.description: Discounted train fares for persons with disabilities and their escorts.
steps: Obtain a Railway Concession Certificate from a government doctor.
Apply for the Photo Identity Card for PWD at the DRM office.
Use the ID card number while booking tickets on IRCTC.
Carry the original ID card during travel.
items.eligibility: Persons with specified orthopedic, visual, or mental disabilities.
items.notes: The concession amount varies based on the type of disability.

items.title: Pollution Under Control (PUC) Certificate
items.description: Mandatory certificate checking the emission levels of a vehicle.
steps: Take the vehicle to an authorized Emission Testing Center.
The technician will test the exhaust using a probe.
If within limits, the data is uploaded to the Vahan portal.
Pay the fee and collect the printed PUC certificate.
items.eligibility: All motor vehicle owners.
items.notes: New vehicles are exempt for the first year. Post that, renewal is every 6-12 months.

items.title: FASTag for Toll Payment
items.description: Electronic toll collection system to avoid queues at highway toll plazas.
steps: Purchase FASTag from a bank, petrol pump, or online (Amazon/Paytm).
Link the tag to your vehicle's RC and a prepaid wallet/bank account.
Fix the tag on the top middle of the windshield from inside.
Ensure sufficient balance for automatic deduction at tolls.
items.eligibility: Owners of four-wheelers and larger vehicles.
items.notes: Driving without FASTag in a FASTag lane results in a double toll penalty.

items.title: Driving Licence Renewal
items.description: Extending the validity of an existing driving licence after expiry.
steps: Apply online on the Sarathi portal within 30 days of expiry.
Submit a Medical Certificate (Form 1A) if aged over 40.
Pay the renewal fee online.
Visit RTO for biometric verification (if required) and receive the card by post.
items.eligibility: Holders of a driving licence nearing or recently expired.
items.notes: If the licence is not renewed within a year of expiry, a re-test may be required.
